sql: SELECT p.product_id, p.name, p.brand, SUM(t.sale_price * t.quantity) AS total_sales, SUM(t.sale_price * t.quantity) - SUM(p.cost_price * t.quantity) AS total_revenue, ((SUM(t.sale_price * t.quantity) - SUM(p.cost_price * t.quantity)) / SUM(t.sale_price * t.quantity)) * 100 AS profit_margin FROM transactions t JOIN products p ON t.product_id = p.product_id WHERE t.txn_timestamp >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '3 months' GROUP BY p.product_id, p.name, p.brand HAVING ((SUM(t.sale_price * t.quantity) - SUM(p.cost_price * t.quantity)) / SUM(t.sale_price * t.quantity)) * 100 > 20 ORDER BY profit_margin DESC

question: Which stores have the highest total sales for products with a discount during the last 2 months, and show the store name, city, and total sales?
sql: SELECT s.store_id, s.city, s.manager_id, SUM(t.sale_price * t.quantity) AS total_sales FROM transactions t JOIN stores s ON t.store_id = s.store_id JOIN promotions p ON t.promo_id = p.promo_id WHERE p.discount_pct > 0 AND t.txn_timestamp >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '2 months' GROUP BY s.store_id, s.city, s.manager_id ORDER BY total_sales DESC

question: Find the products with the highest sales growth rate compared to the same period last year, and show the product name, brand, and sales growth rate.
sql: WITH current_year AS (SELECT p.product_id, p.name, p.brand, SUM(t.sale_price * t.quantity) AS current_year_sales FROM transactions t JOIN products p ON t.product_id = p.product_id WHERE t.txn_timestamp >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year' GROUP BY p.product_id, p.name, p.brand), last_year AS (SELECT p.product_id, p.name, p.brand, SUM(t.sale_price * t.quantity) AS last_year_sales FROM transactions t JOIN products p ON t.product_id = p.product_id WHERE t.txn_timestamp >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '2 years' AND t.txn_timestamp < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year' GROUP BY p.product_id, p.name, p.brand) SELECT cy.product_id, cy.name, cy.brand, ((cy.current_year_sales - ly.last_year_sales) / ly.last_year_sales) * 100 AS sales_growth_rate FROM current_year cy JOIN last_year ly ON cy.product_id = ly.product_id ORDER BY sales_growth_rate DESC LIMIT 5

question: Find the top 5 stores with the highest total sales revenue for the current month, and show their sales volume and revenue by product category
sql: WITH monthly_sales AS (SELECT s.store_id, s.city, p.category, SUM(t.sale_price * t.quantity) AS total_revenue, COUNT(t.txn_id) AS total_sales FROM transactions t JOIN stores s ON t.store_id = s.store_id JOIN products p ON t.product_id = p.product_id WHERE t.txn_timestamp >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) GROUP BY s.store_id, s.city, p.category) SELECT store_id, city, category, total_sales, total_revenue FROM monthly_sales ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 5

question: For each product category, show the total sales volume and revenue for the last quarter, and rank the categories by revenue
sql: WITH quarterly_sales AS (SELECT p.category, SUM(t.sale_price * t.quantity) AS total_revenue, COUNT(t.txn_id) AS total_sales FROM transactions t JOIN products p ON t.product_id = p.product_id WHERE t.txn_timestamp >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '3 months' GROUP BY p.category) SELECT category, total_sales, total_revenue FROM quarterly_sales ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Find the stores with the lowest inventory levels for products in the 'Electronics' category, and show their current inventory quantity and reorder level
sql: WITH low_inventory AS (SELECT s.store_id, s.city, p.name, i.quantity, i.reorder_level FROM inventory i JOIN stores s ON i.store_id = s.store_id JOIN products p ON i.product_id = p.product_id WHERE p.category = 'Electronics' AND i.quantity < i.reorder_level) SELECT store_id, city, name, quantity, reorder_level FROM low_inventory ORDER BY quantity ASC

question: Which products have the highest sales growth rate over the last 2 quarters, and what is their total sales volume and revenue for the current quarter?
sql: WITH quarterly_sales AS (SELECT p.product_id, p.name, SUM(t.sale_price * t.quantity) AS total_revenue, COUNT(t.txn_id) AS total_sales, DATE_TRUNC('quarter', t.txn_timestamp) AS quarter FROM transactions t JOIN products p ON t.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.product_id, p.name, DATE_TRUNC('quarter', t.txn_timestamp)), sales_growth AS (SELECT product_id, name, total_revenue, total_sales, quarter, LAG(total_revenue, 1) OVER (PARTITION BY product_id ORDER BY quarter) AS prev_revenue FROM quarterly_sales) SELECT product_id, name, total_sales, total_revenue FROM sales_growth WHERE quarter = DATE_TRUNC('quarter', CURRENT_DATE) AND total_revenue > prev_revenue ORDER BY (total_revenue - prev_revenue) / prev_revenue DESC

question: For each store, show their total sales revenue and volume for the current month, and rank the stores by revenue
sql: WITH monthly_sales AS (SELECT s.store_id, s.city, SUM(t.sale_price * t.quantity) AS total_revenue, COUNT(t.txn_id) AS total_sales FROM transactions t JOIN stores s ON t.store_id = s.store_id WHERE t.txn_timestamp >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) GROUP BY s.store_id, s.city) SELECT store_id, city, total_sales, total_revenue FROM monthly_sales ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Find the products with the highest average sale price across all stores for the last quarter, and show their total sales volume and revenue for the quarter
sql: WITH quarterly_sales AS (SELECT p.product_id, p.name, SUM(t.sale_price * t.quantity) AS total_revenue, COUNT(t.txn_id) AS total_sales, AVG(t.sale_price) AS avg_sale_price FROM transactions t JOIN products p ON t.product_id = p.product_id WHERE t.txn_timestamp >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '3 months' GROUP BY p.product_id, p.name) SELECT product_id, name, total_sales, total_revenue FROM quarterly_sales ORDER BY avg_sale_price DESC

question: Find the products with the highest sales growth rate over the last 2 weeks, considering only products with at least 10 transactions.
sql: WITH weekly_sales AS (SELECT p.product_id, p.name, DATE_TRUNC('week', t.txn_timestamp) AS week, SUM(t.quantity * t.sale_price) AS weekly_revenue, COUNT(t.txn_id) AS weekly_transactions FROM transactions t JOIN products p ON t.product_id = p.product_id WHERE t.txn_timestamp >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '14 days' GROUP BY p.product_id, p.name, DATE_TRUNC('week', t.txn_timestamp)), growth_rate AS (SELECT ws.product_id, ws.name, (ws.weekly_revenue - LAG(ws.weekly_revenue, 1) OVER (PARTITION BY ws.product_id ORDER BY ws.week)) / LAG(ws.weekly_revenue, 1) OVER (PARTITION BY ws.product_id ORDER BY ws.week) AS growth FROM weekly_sales ws) SELECT gr.product_id, gr.name, gr.growth FROM growth_rate gr WHERE gr.growth IS NOT NULL AND gr.growth > 0 AND gr.product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM weekly_sales GROUP BY product_id HAVING SUM(weekly_transactions) >= 10) ORDER BY gr.growth DESC

question: How many active contracts are there for each industry, and what is the total ARR for each industry?
sql: SELECT a.industry, COUNT(c.contract_id) AS contract_count, SUM(a.arr) AS total_arr FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status IN ('active', 'renewed') GROUP BY a.industry ORDER BY contract_count DESC

question: Find the top 10 features used by enterprise accounts, ranked by total session seconds.
sql: SELECT f.name AS feature_name, SUM(fu.session_seconds) AS total_session_seconds FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id JOIN accounts a ON u.account_id = a.account_id JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status = 'active' AND f.tier = 'enterprise' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY total_session_seconds DESC LIMIT 10

question: Which accounts have the highest total ARR and the most users, and what is their industry?
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, a.industry, COUNT(u.user_id) AS user_count, SUM(a.arr) AS total_arr FROM accounts a JOIN users u ON a.account_id = u.account_id GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name, a.industry ORDER BY total_arr DESC, user_count DESC

question: Show the number of contracts and total MRR for each account, filtered by active contracts.
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, COUNT(c.contract_id) AS contract_count, SUM(c.mrr) AS total_mrr FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status IN ('active', 'renewed') GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name ORDER BY contract_count DESC

question: What is the average employee count and ARR for each industry, and how many accounts are in each industry?
sql: SELECT a.industry, AVG(a.employee_count) AS avg_employee_count, AVG(a.arr) AS avg_arr, COUNT(a.account_id) AS account_count FROM accounts a GROUP BY a.industry ORDER BY account_count DESC

question: Find the top 5 features with the most usage in the last 30 days, ranked by usage count.
sql: SELECT f.name AS feature_name, COUNT(fu.usage_id) AS usage_count FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id WHERE fu.used_at >= CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY usage_count DESC LIMIT 5

question: Which accounts have the highest renewal probability and the most contracts, and what is their industry?
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, a.industry, COUNT(c.contract_id) AS contract_count, AVG(c.renewal_probability) AS avg_renewal_probability FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name, a.industry ORDER BY avg_renewal_probability DESC, contract_count DESC

question: Show the total ARR and number of users for each account, filtered by accounts with more than 100 employees.
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, SUM(a.arr) AS total_arr, COUNT(u.user_id) AS user_count FROM accounts a JOIN users u ON a.account_id = u.account_id WHERE a.employee_count > 100 GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name ORDER BY total_arr DESC

question: Which accounts have the highest total ARR and how many users do they have?
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, SUM(a.arr) AS total_arr, COUNT(u.user_id) AS user_count FROM accounts a JOIN users u ON a.account_id = u.account_id GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name ORDER BY total_arr DESC

question: Show the number of active users and total session seconds for each feature in the premium tier
sql: SELECT f.feature_id, f.name, COUNT(fu.usage_id) AS usage_count, SUM(fu.session_seconds) AS total_session_seconds FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id WHERE f.tier = 'premium' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY usage_count DESC

question: Find the average MRR and contract count for accounts in the technology industry
sql: SELECT a.industry, AVG(c.mrr) AS avg_mrr, COUNT(c.contract_id) AS contract_count FROM contracts c JOIN accounts a ON c.account_id = a.account_id WHERE a.industry = 'technology' AND c.status IN ('active', 'renewed') GROUP BY a.industry

question: Which users have used features in the last 30 days and how many sessions do they have?
sql: SELECT u.user_id, u.email, COUNT(fu.usage_id) AS session_count FROM feature_usage fu JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id WHERE fu.used_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY u.user_id, u.email ORDER BY session_count DESC

question: Show the total ARR and employee count for accounts with active contracts
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, SUM(a.arr) AS total_arr, SUM(a.employee_count) AS total_employees FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status IN ('active', 'renewed') GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name ORDER BY total_arr DESC

question: Find the features used most by admin users in the last 60 days
sql: SELECT f.feature_id, f.name, COUNT(fu.usage_id) AS usage_count FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id WHERE u.is_admin = TRUE AND fu.used_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '60 days' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY usage_count DESC

question: Which accounts have the highest renewal probability and how many contracts do they have?
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, AVG(c.renewal_probability) AS avg_renewal_probability, COUNT(c.contract_id) AS contract_count FROM contracts c JOIN accounts a ON c.account_id = a.account_id WHERE c.status IN ('active', 'renewed') GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name ORDER BY avg_renewal_probability DESC

question: Show the total session seconds and user count for each feature tier
sql: SELECT f.tier, COUNT(fu.usage_id) AS usage_count, SUM(fu.session_seconds) AS total_session_seconds FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id GROUP BY f.tier ORDER BY usage_count DESC

question: How many active accounts are there in each industry, along with their total ARR?
sql: SELECT a.industry, COUNT(a.account_id) AS account_count, SUM(a.arr) AS total_arr FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status IN ('active', 'renewed') GROUP BY a.industry ORDER BY total_arr DESC

question: Which features are used most by users in the 'admin' role?
sql: SELECT f.name AS feature_name, COUNT(fu.usage_id) AS usage_count FROM feature_usage fu JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id WHERE u.role = 'admin' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY usage_count DESC

question: Find the top 5 accounts with the highest employee count, along with their company name and ARR
sql: SELECT a.company_name, a.employee_count, a.arr FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status IN ('active', 'renewed') ORDER BY a.employee_count DESC LIMIT 5

question: Show the total MRR for each account, along with their company name and industry
sql: SELECT a.company_name, a.industry, SUM(c.mrr) AS total_mrr FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status IN ('active', 'renewed') GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name, a.industry ORDER BY total_mrr DESC

question: Which users have used the most features in the last 30 days?
sql: SELECT u.user_id, u.email, COUNT(DISTINCT fu.feature_id) AS feature_count FROM feature_usage fu JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id WHERE fu.used_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY u.user_id, u.email ORDER BY feature_count DESC

question: Find the average session seconds for each feature, along with their name and tier
sql: SELECT f.name AS feature_name, f.tier, AVG(fu.session_seconds) AS avg_session_seconds FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name, f.tier ORDER BY avg_session_seconds DESC

question: Show the total ARR for each industry, along with the number of active accounts
sql: SELECT a.industry, COUNT(a.account_id) AS account_count, SUM(a.arr) AS total_arr FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status IN ('active', 'renewed') GROUP BY a.industry ORDER BY total_arr DESC

question: Which accounts have the highest renewal probability, along with their company name and MRR?
sql: SELECT a.company_name, c.mrr, c.renewal_probability FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status IN ('active', 'renewed') ORDER BY c.renewal_probability DESC LIMIT 5

question: How many active users are there for each account?
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, COUNT(u.user_id) AS active_user_count FROM accounts a JOIN users u ON a.account_id = u.account_id WHERE u.last_login >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name ORDER BY active_user_count DESC

question: Find the total revenue for each account with a contract status of 'active' or 'renewed'
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, SUM(c.mrr) AS total_revenue FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status IN ('active', 'renewed') GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Which features are used most frequently by users with an 'admin' role?
sql: SELECT f.name AS feature_name, COUNT(fu.usage_id) AS usage_count FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id WHERE u.role = 'admin' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY usage_count DESC

question: Show the number of contracts and total MRR for each industry
sql: SELECT a.industry, COUNT(c.contract_id) AS contract_count, SUM(c.mrr) AS total_mrr FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status = 'active' GROUP BY a.industry ORDER BY total_mrr DESC

question: Find the average session seconds for each feature used by enterprise accounts
sql: SELECT f.name AS feature_name, AVG(fu.session_seconds) AS avg_session_seconds FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id JOIN accounts a ON u.account_id = a.account_id WHERE a.industry = 'enterprise' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY avg_session_seconds DESC

question: Which accounts have the highest renewal probability?
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, c.renewal_probability FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status = 'active' ORDER BY c.renewal_probability DESC

question: How many users are there for each account with a contract status of 'active' or 'pending'
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, COUNT(u.user_id) AS user_count FROM accounts a JOIN users u ON a.account_id = u.account_id JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status IN ('active', 'pending') GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name ORDER BY user_count DESC

question: Find the total ARR for each industry with a contract status of 'active'
sql: SELECT a.industry, SUM(a.arr) AS total_arr FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status = 'active' GROUP BY a.industry ORDER BY total_arr DESC

question: Which enterprise accounts have the highest total ARR and the most active users?
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, SUM(a.arr) AS total_arr, COUNT(DISTINCT u.user_id) AS active_user_count FROM accounts a JOIN users u ON a.account_id = u.account_id WHERE a.industry = 'enterprise' AND u.last_login >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name ORDER BY total_arr DESC, active_user_count DESC

question: Find the top features used by accounts with high renewal probability and active contracts.
sql: SELECT f.feature_id, f.name, COUNT(fu.usage_id) AS usage_count, AVG(c.renewal_probability) AS avg_renewal_prob FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id JOIN accounts a ON u.account_id = a.account_id JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status IN ('active', 'renewed') AND c.renewal_probability > 0.5 GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY usage_count DESC, avg_renewal_prob DESC

question: Show the total MRR and count of contracts for each account with active contracts and high employee count.
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, SUM(c.mrr) AS total_mrr, COUNT(c.contract_id) AS contract_count FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status IN ('active', 'renewed') AND a.employee_count > 100 GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name ORDER BY total_mrr DESC, contract_count DESC

question: Which features have the highest average session time and are used by accounts with high ARR?
sql: SELECT f.feature_id, f.name, AVG(fu.session_seconds) AS avg_session_time, AVG(a.arr) AS avg_arr FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id JOIN accounts a ON u.account_id = a.account_id WHERE a.arr > 10000 GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY avg_session_time DESC, avg_arr DESC

question: Find the accounts with the most active users and the highest total ARR in the last quarter.
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, COUNT(DISTINCT u.user_id) AS active_user_count, SUM(a.arr) AS total_arr FROM accounts a JOIN users u ON a.account_id = u.account_id WHERE u.last_login >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days' AND a.created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year' GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name ORDER BY active_user_count DESC, total_arr DESC

question: Which features are used most by accounts in the technology industry with active contracts?
sql: SELECT f.feature_id, f.name, COUNT(fu.usage_id) AS usage_count FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id JOIN accounts a ON u.account_id = a.account_id JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status IN ('active', 'renewed') AND a.industry = 'technology' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY usage_count DESC

question: Show the total session time and count of feature usage for each account with high renewal probability.
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, SUM(fu.session_seconds) AS total_session_time, COUNT(fu.usage_id) AS usage_count FROM feature_usage fu JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id JOIN accounts a ON u.account_id = a.account_id JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.renewal_probability > 0.5 GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name ORDER BY total_session_time DESC, usage_count DESC

question: Find the top accounts with the highest total MRR and the most contracts in the last year.
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, SUM(c.mrr) AS total_mrr, COUNT(c.contract_id) AS contract_count FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.start_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year' GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name ORDER BY total_mrr DESC, contract_count DESC

question: Which features have the highest average session duration for enterprise accounts in the last 30 days?
sql: SELECT f.name AS feature_name, AVG(fu.session_seconds) AS avg_session_duration FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id JOIN accounts a ON u.account_id = a.account_id JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status = 'active' AND f.tier = 'enterprise' AND fu.used_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY avg_session_duration DESC

question: Find the top 5 accounts with the highest total ARR and their corresponding industry, sorted by total ARR in descending order
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, a.industry, SUM(a.arr) AS total_arr FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status IN ('active', 'renewed') GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name, a.industry ORDER BY total_arr DESC LIMIT 5

question: Show the number of active users and total session duration for each feature, filtered by features released in the last 6 months
sql: SELECT f.name AS feature_name, COUNT(DISTINCT fu.user_id) AS active_users, SUM(fu.session_seconds) AS total_session_duration FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id WHERE f.release_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '6 months' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY total_session_duration DESC

question: Which accounts have the highest average MRR and are likely to renew their contracts, based on renewal probability?
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, AVG(c.mrr) AS avg_mrr, c.renewal_probability FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status IN ('active', 'renewed') AND c.renewal_probability > 0.5 GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name, c.renewal_probability ORDER BY avg_mrr DESC

question: Find the features with the lowest usage count and average session duration for small business accounts in the last 90 days
sql: SELECT f.name AS feature_name, COUNT(fu.usage_id) AS usage_count, AVG(fu.session_seconds) AS avg_session_duration FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id JOIN accounts a ON u.account_id = a.account_id WHERE a.employee_count < 50 AND fu.used_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY usage_count ASC, avg_session_duration ASC

question: Show the total ARR and number of contracts for each industry, filtered by accounts with more than 100 employees
sql: SELECT a.industry, SUM(a.arr) AS total_arr, COUNT(c.contract_id) AS contract_count FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE a.employee_count > 100 AND c.status IN ('active', 'renewed') GROUP BY a.industry ORDER BY total_arr DESC

question: Which features have the highest average session duration for power users, defined as users with more than 10 sessions in the last 30 days?
sql: SELECT f.name AS feature_name, AVG(fu.session_seconds) AS avg_session_duration FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id WHERE fu.used_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' AND u.user_id IN (SELECT user_id FROM feature_usage WHERE used_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY user_id HAVING COUNT(usage_id) > 10) GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY avg_session_duration DESC

question: Find the accounts with the highest total session duration and average MRR, filtered by accounts with a high renewal probability
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, SUM(fu.session_seconds) AS total_session_duration, AVG(c.mrr) AS avg_mrr FROM feature_usage fu JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id JOIN accounts a ON u.account_id = a.account_id JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.renewal_probability > 0.7 AND c.status IN ('active', 'renewed') GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name ORDER BY total_session_duration DESC, avg_mrr DESC

question: Find the top features used by admin users in the last 30 days, along with their usage count and total session seconds
sql: SELECT f.feature_id, f.name, COUNT(fu.usage_id) AS usage_count, SUM(fu.session_seconds) AS total_session_seconds FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id WHERE u.is_admin = TRUE AND fu.used_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY usage_count DESC

question: Show the total MRR and number of contracts for each account, filtered by active contracts and sorted by MRR in descending order
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, SUM(c.mrr) AS total_mrr, COUNT(c.contract_id) AS contract_count FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status = 'active' GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name ORDER BY total_mrr DESC

question: Which features have the highest average session seconds per user, and what is their total usage count?
sql: SELECT f.feature_id, f.name, AVG(fu.session_seconds) AS avg_session_seconds, COUNT(fu.usage_id) AS total_usage_count FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY avg_session_seconds DESC

question: Find the top accounts by total ARR, along with their industry and employee count, filtered by accounts with more than 100 employees
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, a.industry, a.employee_count, SUM(a.arr) AS total_arr FROM accounts a WHERE a.employee_count > 100 GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name, a.industry, a.employee_count ORDER BY total_arr DESC

question: Show the total revenue and number of users for each tier, filtered by features released in the last 6 months
sql: SELECT f.tier, SUM(fu.session_seconds) AS total_revenue, COUNT(u.user_id) AS user_count FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id WHERE f.release_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '6 months' GROUP BY f.tier ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Which accounts have the highest renewal probability, and what is their total MRR and contract count?
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, AVG(c.renewal_probability) AS avg_renewal_probability, SUM(c.mrr) AS total_mrr, COUNT(c.contract_id) AS contract_count FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status = 'active' GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name ORDER BY avg_renewal_probability DESC

question: Find the top features used by non-admin users in the last 90 days, along with their usage count and total session seconds
sql: SELECT f.feature_id, f.name, COUNT(fu.usage_id) AS usage_count, SUM(fu.session_seconds) AS total_session_seconds FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id WHERE u.is_admin = FALSE AND fu.used_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY usage_count DESC

question: Which enterprise accounts have the highest total ARR and the most active users in the last 30 days?
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, SUM(a.arr) AS total_arr, COUNT(DISTINCT u.user_id) AS active_users FROM accounts a JOIN users u ON a.account_id = u.account_id WHERE a.industry = 'enterprise' AND u.last_login >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name ORDER BY total_arr DESC, active_users DESC

question: Find the top features used by admin users in the last quarter, along with their usage count and average session duration.
sql: SELECT f.feature_id, f.name, COUNT(fu.usage_id) AS usage_count, AVG(fu.session_seconds) AS avg_session_duration FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id WHERE u.is_admin = TRUE AND fu.used_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '3 months' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY usage_count DESC

question: Show the total MRR and contract count for each account with active contracts, filtered by renewal probability above 0.5.
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, SUM(c.mrr) AS total_mrr, COUNT(c.contract_id) AS contract_count FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status = 'active' AND c.renewal_probability > 0.5 GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name ORDER BY total_mrr DESC

question: Which features have the highest average session duration and are used by more than 10 users in the last 6 months?
sql: SELECT f.feature_id, f.name, AVG(fu.session_seconds) AS avg_session_duration, COUNT(DISTINCT fu.user_id) AS user_count FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id WHERE fu.used_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '6 months' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT fu.user_id) > 10 ORDER BY avg_session_duration DESC

question: Find the accounts with the highest total ARR growth in the last year, considering only accounts with more than 100 employees.
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, SUM(a.arr) AS total_arr, (SUM(a.arr) - LAG(SUM(a.arr)) OVER (ORDER BY a.account_id)) AS arr_growth FROM accounts a WHERE a.employee_count > 100 AND a.created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year' GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name ORDER BY arr_growth DESC

question: Show the top 10 features with the most usage in the last month, along with their usage count and average session duration.
sql: SELECT f.feature_id, f.name, COUNT(fu.usage_id) AS usage_count, AVG(fu.session_seconds) AS avg_session_duration FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id WHERE fu.used_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 month' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY usage_count DESC LIMIT 10

question: Which accounts have the highest average contract MRR and more than 2 active contracts?
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, AVG(c.mrr) AS avg_mrr, COUNT(c.contract_id) AS contract_count FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status = 'active' GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name HAVING COUNT(c.contract_id) > 2 ORDER BY avg_mrr DESC

question: Which features have the highest average session time for enterprise accounts in the last 30 days?
sql: SELECT f.name AS feature_name, AVG(fu.session_seconds) AS avg_session_time FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id JOIN accounts a ON u.account_id = a.account_id JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status = 'active' AND f.tier = 'enterprise' AND fu.used_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY avg_session_time DESC

question: For each account, show their company name, total number of active users, and total ARR
sql: SELECT a.company_name, COUNT(u.user_id) AS active_users, SUM(a.arr) AS total_arr FROM accounts a JOIN users u ON a.account_id = u.account_id WHERE u.last_login >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name ORDER BY total_arr DESC

question: Which features are used most frequently by admin users in the last 60 days?
sql: SELECT f.name AS feature_name, COUNT(fu.usage_id) AS usage_count FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id WHERE u.is_admin = TRUE AND fu.used_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '60 days' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY usage_count DESC

question: Show the top 10 accounts by total MRR, along with their company name and industry
sql: SELECT a.company_name, a.industry, SUM(c.mrr) AS total_mrr FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status = 'active' GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name, a.industry ORDER BY total_mrr DESC LIMIT 10

question: For each feature, show its name, total number of users, and average session time for users in the 'growth' tier
sql: SELECT f.name AS feature_name, COUNT(DISTINCT u.user_id) AS total_users, AVG(fu.session_seconds) AS avg_session_time FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id JOIN accounts a ON u.account_id = a.account_id WHERE a.industry = 'growth' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY total_users DESC

question: Show the top 5 features by total session time for accounts in the 'technology' industry
sql: SELECT f.name AS feature_name, SUM(fu.session_seconds) AS total_session_time FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id JOIN accounts a ON u.account_id = a.account_id WHERE a.industry = 'technology' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY total_session_time DESC LIMIT 5

question: For each contract, show its status, account company name, and total MRR, filtered by contracts with a renewal probability above 0.5
sql: SELECT c.status, a.company_name, SUM(c.mrr) AS total_mrr FROM contracts c JOIN accounts a ON c.account_id = a.account_id WHERE c.renewal_probability > 0.5 AND c.status IN ('active', 'renewed') GROUP BY c.contract_id, c.status, a.company_name ORDER BY total_mrr DESC

question: Which features have the highest average session time for users in the technology industry?
sql: SELECT f.name AS feature_name, AVG(fu.session_seconds) AS avg_session_time FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id JOIN accounts a ON u.account_id = a.account_id WHERE a.industry = 'technology' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY avg_session_time DESC

question: Find the top 5 accounts with the highest total ARR, considering only active contracts and including the company name and employee count.
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, a.employee_count, SUM(c.mrr) AS total_arr FROM contracts c JOIN accounts a ON c.account_id = a.account_id WHERE c.status = 'active' GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name, a.employee_count ORDER BY total_arr DESC LIMIT 5

question: Show the number of users and total ARR for each industry, filtering by active contracts and including the industry name.
sql: SELECT a.industry, COUNT(DISTINCT u.user_id) AS user_count, SUM(c.mrr) AS total_arr FROM contracts c JOIN accounts a ON c.account_id = a.account_id JOIN users u ON a.account_id = u.account_id WHERE c.status = 'active' GROUP BY a.industry ORDER BY total_arr DESC

question: Which features are used most frequently by admin users in the last 30 days, including the feature name and usage count?
sql: SELECT f.name AS feature_name, COUNT(fu.usage_id) AS usage_count FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id WHERE u.is_admin = TRUE AND fu.used_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY usage_count DESC

question: Find the average session time and total usage count for each feature, considering only active contracts and including the feature name.
sql: SELECT f.name AS feature_name, AVG(fu.session_seconds) AS avg_session_time, COUNT(fu.usage_id) AS total_usage FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id JOIN accounts a ON u.account_id = a.account_id JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status = 'active' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY avg_session_time DESC

question: Show the top 3 accounts with the highest renewal probability, including the company name and contract status.
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, c.status, c.renewal_probability FROM contracts c JOIN accounts a ON c.account_id = a.account_id WHERE c.status = 'active' ORDER BY c.renewal_probability DESC LIMIT 3

question: Which industries have the highest average ARR per user, considering only active contracts and including the industry name?
sql: SELECT a.industry, AVG(c.mrr) AS avg_arr_per_user, COUNT(DISTINCT u.user_id) AS user_count FROM contracts c JOIN accounts a ON c.account_id = a.account_id JOIN users u ON a.account_id = u.account_id WHERE c.status = 'active' GROUP BY a.industry ORDER BY avg_arr_per_user DESC

question: Find the total ARR and user count for each tier of features, including the tier name and filtering by active contracts.
sql: SELECT f.tier, SUM(c.mrr) AS total_arr, COUNT(DISTINCT u.user_id) AS user_count FROM contracts c JOIN accounts a ON c.account_id = a.account_id JOIN users u ON a.account_id = u.account_id JOIN feature_usage fu ON u.user_id = fu.user_id JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id WHERE c.status = 'active' GROUP BY f.tier ORDER BY total_arr DESC

question: Which enterprise accounts have the highest total ARR, and what are their average contract MRR and employee counts?
sql: SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, SUM(c.mrr) AS total_mrr, AVG(c.mrr) AS avg_mrr, AVG(a.employee_count) AS avg_employees, SUM(a.arr) AS total_arr FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status IN ('active', 'renewed') AND a.industry = 'enterprise' GROUP BY a.account_id, a.company_name ORDER BY total_arr DESC

question: Find the top 5 features used by admins in the last 30 days, along with their usage counts and average session seconds.
sql: SELECT f.feature_id, f.name, COUNT(fu.usage_id) AS usage_count, AVG(fu.session_seconds) AS avg_session_seconds FROM feature_usage fu JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id WHERE u.is_admin = TRUE AND fu.used_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name ORDER BY usage_count DESC LIMIT 5

question: For each account tier, show the total ARR, average contract MRR, and count of active contracts.
sql: SELECT a.industry AS account_tier, SUM(a.arr) AS total_arr, AVG(c.mrr) AS avg_mrr, COUNT(c.contract_id) AS active_contracts FROM accounts a JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id WHERE c.status IN ('active', 'renewed') GROUP BY a.industry ORDER BY total_arr DESC

question: For each customer, show their email and total number of active subscriptions.
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS total_subscriptions FROM customers c LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id AND s.status IN ('active', 'trialing') GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email ORDER BY total_subscriptions DESC

question: Find the total revenue generated by each plan through their subscriptions.
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, SUM(i.amount) AS plan_revenue FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY plan_revenue DESC

question: For each customer, show their email and total number of shipments in the last 30 days.
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS total_shipments FROM customers c LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id LEFT JOIN shipments sh ON s.sub_id = sh.sub_id WHERE sh.shipped_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email ORDER BY total_shipments DESC

question: Which customers have an average invoice amount above 50?
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, AVG(i.amount) AS avg_invoice FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email HAVING AVG(i.amount) > 50 ORDER BY avg_invoice DESC

question: Find the total number of active subscriptions and total revenue for each plan.
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS total_subscriptions, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'trialing') AND i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: For each customer, show their email and total number of invoices paid in the last 30 days.
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, COUNT(i.invoice_id) AS total_invoices FROM customers c LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id LEFT JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.paid_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' AND i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email ORDER BY total_invoices DESC

question: Which plans have a subscription retention rate above 75%?
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, COUNT(CASE WHEN s.end_date IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) AS active_subscriptions, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS total_subscriptions, COUNT(CASE WHEN s.end_date IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) * 1.0 / COUNT(s.sub_id) AS retention_rate FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name HAVING COUNT(CASE WHEN s.end_date IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) * 1.0 / COUNT(s.sub_id) > 0.75 ORDER BY retention_rate DESC

question: How many active subscriptions does each customer have, along with their email and total lifetime value?
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS active_subscriptions, c.ltv AS total_ltv FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'trialing') GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email, c.ltv ORDER BY active_subscriptions DESC

question: Find the total number of shipments and total revenue generated by each plan, along with its name and price.
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, p.price_monthly, COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS total_shipments, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN shipments sh ON s.sub_id = sh.sub_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name, p.price_monthly ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Which customers have the highest total lifetime value and at least one active subscription?
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, c.ltv AS total_ltv FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'trialing') GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email, c.ltv HAVING c.ltv > 1000 ORDER BY total_ltv DESC

question: Show the number of new customers acquired in the last 30 days, along with their referral source and total lifetime value.
sql: SELECT c.referral_source, COUNT(c.customer_id) AS new_customers, SUM(c.ltv) AS total_ltv FROM customers c WHERE c.signup_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY c.referral_source ORDER BY new_customers DESC

question: Find the total revenue generated by each customer through their subscriptions, along with their email and total lifetime value.
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, c.ltv AS total_ltv, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email, c.ltv ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: How many subscriptions are currently in the 'trialing' status, and what is the total revenue generated by these subscriptions?
sql: SELECT COUNT(s.sub_id) AS trialing_subscriptions, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue FROM subscriptions s JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE s.status = 'trialing' AND i.status IN ('paid', 'settled')

question: Which plans have the highest average revenue per subscription, along with their name and price?
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, p.price_monthly, AVG(i.amount) AS avg_revenue FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name, p.price_monthly ORDER BY avg_revenue DESC

question: Find the total number of shipments and total revenue generated by each customer in the last 60 days, along with their email and total lifetime value.
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, c.ltv AS total_ltv, COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS total_shipments, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN shipments sh ON s.sub_id = sh.sub_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE sh.shipped_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '60 days' AND i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email, c.ltv ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: How many active customers do we have, and what is the total lifetime value of these customers?
sql: SELECT COUNT(c.customer_id) AS active_customers, SUM(c.ltv) AS total_ltv FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'trial')

question: Find the total number of shipments made to each customer, along with the customer's email and signup date.
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, c.signup_date, COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS total_shipments FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN shipments sh ON s.sub_id = sh.sub_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email, c.signup_date

question: Show the plan name and the total number of subscriptions for each plan, including the plan price.
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, p.price_monthly, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS total_subscriptions FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'trial') GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name, p.price_monthly

question: Which customers have an average invoice amount above 50, and what is their total invoice amount?
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, AVG(i.amount) AS avg_invoice_amount, SUM(i.amount) AS total_invoice_amount FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email HAVING AVG(i.amount) > 50

question: Find the total revenue generated by each plan, including the plan name and price.
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, p.price_monthly, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name, p.price_monthly

question: How many customers have signed up in the last 30 days, and what is the total lifetime value of these customers?
sql: SELECT COUNT(c.customer_id) AS new_customers, SUM(c.ltv) AS total_ltv FROM customers c WHERE c.signup_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'

question: Show the customer's email and the total number of invoices for each customer, including the total invoice amount.
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, COUNT(i.invoice_id) AS total_invoices, SUM(i.amount) AS total_invoice_amount FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email

question: Find the plan with the highest average invoice amount, including the plan name and price.
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, p.price_monthly, AVG(i.amount) AS avg_invoice_amount FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name, p.price_monthly ORDER BY avg_invoice_amount DESC LIMIT 1

question: Find the total number of shipments and revenue generated by each plan, along with its name and price?
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, p.price_monthly, COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS total_shipments, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN shipments sh ON s.sub_id = sh.sub_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name, p.price_monthly ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Which customers have the highest lifetime value and at least one active subscription?
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, c.ltv, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS active_subscriptions FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'pending') GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email, c.ltv HAVING c.ltv > 1000 ORDER BY c.ltv DESC

question: Show the total amount of invoices issued and paid for each customer, along with their email and referral source?
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, c.referral_source, COUNT(i.invoice_id) AS total_invoices, SUM(i.amount) AS total_amount FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.status IN ('paid', 'issued') GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email, c.referral_source ORDER BY total_amount DESC

question: Find the average monthly price of plans with at least one subscription, along with the plan name and total number of subscriptions?
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, AVG(p.price_monthly) AS avg_price, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS total_subscriptions FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name HAVING COUNT(s.sub_id) > 0 ORDER BY avg_price DESC

question: How many customers have signed up through each referral source, along with the total lifetime value of these customers?
sql: SELECT c.referral_source, COUNT(c.customer_id) AS total_customers, SUM(c.ltv) AS total_ltv FROM customers c GROUP BY c.referral_source ORDER BY total_ltv DESC

question: Which plans have the highest total revenue and at least one paid invoice?
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name HAVING SUM(i.amount) > 0 ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Find the total number of shipments and delivery rate for each carrier, along with the total number of tracking numbers?
sql: SELECT sh.carrier, COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS total_shipments, COUNT(CASE WHEN sh.delivered_date IS NOT NULL THEN sh.shipment_id END) AS delivered_shipments, COUNT(sh.tracking_number) AS total_tracking_numbers FROM shipments sh GROUP BY sh.carrier ORDER BY total_shipments DESC

question: For each customer, show their email and total number of subscriptions.
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS total_subscriptions FROM customers c LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email ORDER BY total_subscriptions DESC

question: Which plans have an average subscription price above 50?
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, AVG(p.price_monthly) AS avg_price FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name HAVING AVG(p.price_monthly) > 50 ORDER BY avg_price DESC

question: Show the total number of active subscriptions and their corresponding total monthly revenue for each plan, ordered by revenue in descending order
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS total_subscriptions, SUM(p.price_monthly) AS total_revenue FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'trialing') GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Find the average lifetime value of customers who have subscribed to each plan, along with the total number of customers and total revenue generated by each plan
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, AVG(c.ltv) AS avg_ltv, COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS total_customers, SUM(p.price_monthly) AS total_revenue FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'trialing') GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Which plans have the highest retention rate, calculated as the number of subscriptions that are still active after 3 months, divided by the total number of subscriptions
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, COUNT(CASE WHEN s.end_date IS NULL AND s.start_date < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '3 months' THEN s.sub_id END) AS retained_subscriptions, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS total_subscriptions, COUNT(CASE WHEN s.end_date IS NULL AND s.start_date < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '3 months' THEN s.sub_id END) * 1.0 / COUNT(s.sub_id) AS retention_rate FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'trialing') GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY retention_rate DESC

question: Find the total revenue generated by each plan over the last 6 months, along with the total number of subscriptions and average monthly revenue per subscription
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue, COUNT(DISTINCT s.sub_id) AS total_subscriptions, SUM(i.amount) * 1.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT s.sub_id) AS avg_revenue_per_subscription FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.issued_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '6 months' AND i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Show the total number of shipments made for each plan over the last 3 months, along with the total number of subscriptions and average number of shipments per subscription
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, COUNT(DISTINCT sh.shipment_id) AS total_shipments, COUNT(DISTINCT s.sub_id) AS total_subscriptions, COUNT(DISTINCT sh.shipment_id) * 1.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT s.sub_id) AS avg_shipments_per_subscription FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN shipments sh ON s.sub_id = sh.sub_id WHERE sh.shipped_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '3 months' GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY total_shipments DESC

question: Find the average number of days between shipments for each plan, along with the total number of shipments and total number of subscriptions
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, AVG(sh2.shipped_date - sh1.shipped_date) AS avg_days_between_shipments, COUNT(DISTINCT sh1.shipment_id) AS total_shipments, COUNT(DISTINCT s.sub_id) AS total_subscriptions FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN shipments sh1 ON s.sub_id = sh1.sub_id JOIN shipments sh2 ON s.sub_id = sh2.sub_id AND sh2.shipped_date > sh1.shipped_date WHERE sh1.shipped_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '6 months' GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY avg_days_between_shipments ASC

question: Which customers have the highest total lifetime value, along with their referral source and total number of subscriptions
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, c.referral_source, SUM(p.price_monthly) AS total_ltv, COUNT(DISTINCT s.sub_id) AS total_subscriptions FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN plans p ON s.plan_id = p.plan_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'trialing') GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email, c.referral_source ORDER BY total_ltv DESC

question: Find the total revenue generated by each referral source over the last 12 months, along with the total number of customers and average revenue per customer
sql: SELECT c.referral_source, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue, COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS total_customers, SUM(i.amount) * 1.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS avg_revenue_per_customer FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.issued_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '12 months' AND i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY c.referral_source ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Find the total revenue generated by each plan and the number of active subscriptions for that plan.
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS active_subscriptions FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'paused') AND i.status IN ('paid', 'pending') GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Show the top 5 customers with the highest average invoice amount and their corresponding subscription plan.
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, p.name, AVG(i.amount) AS avg_invoice_amount FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN plans p ON s.plan_id = p.plan_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.status IN ('paid', 'pending') GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email, p.name ORDER BY avg_invoice_amount DESC LIMIT 5

question: Find the total revenue generated by each customer through their subscriptions and the number of shipments they have received.
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue, COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS total_shipments FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id JOIN shipments sh ON s.sub_id = sh.sub_id WHERE i.status IN ('paid', 'pending') AND sh.delivered_date IS NOT NULL GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Which customers have an active subscription and have not received a shipment in the last 30 days?
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, s.sub_id, p.name FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN plans p ON s.plan_id = p.plan_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'paused') AND s.sub_id NOT IN (SELECT sub_id FROM shipments WHERE delivered_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days') ORDER BY c.customer_id

question: Find the total revenue generated by each plan in the last 60 days and the number of new subscriptions for that plan during the same period.
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue, COUNT(DISTINCT s.sub_id) AS new_subscriptions FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.issued_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '60 days' AND s.start_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '60 days' GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Find the total revenue generated by each plan through their subscriptions, including the plan name and the number of active subscriptions.
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS active_subscriptions, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE s.status = 'active' AND i.status = 'paid' GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Show the average lifetime value of customers who have subscribed to each plan, including the plan name and the number of customers.
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, AVG(c.ltv) AS avg_ltv, COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS num_customers FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE s.status = 'active' GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY avg_ltv DESC

question: Which plans have the highest average monthly revenue per subscription, including the plan name and the average revenue?
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, AVG(i.amount) AS avg_revenue FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE s.status = 'active' AND i.status = 'paid' GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name HAVING AVG(i.amount) > 50 ORDER BY avg_revenue DESC

question: Find the total number of shipments made for each plan, including the plan name and the number of shipments.
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS total_shipments FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN shipments sh ON s.sub_id = sh.sub_id WHERE s.status = 'active' GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY total_shipments DESC

question: Show the total revenue generated by each customer, including their email and lifetime value, for customers who have made at least one payment.
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, c.ltv, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.status = 'paid' GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email, c.ltv HAVING SUM(i.amount) > 0 ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Find the average time to delivery for each plan, including the plan name and the average delivery time.
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, AVG(sh.delivered_date - sh.shipped_date) AS avg_delivery_time FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN shipments sh ON s.sub_id = sh.sub_id WHERE s.status = 'active' GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY avg_delivery_time ASC

question: Which customers have the highest total revenue, including their email and lifetime value, for customers who have made at least one payment in the last 30 days?
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, c.ltv, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.status = 'paid' AND i.paid_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email, c.ltv HAVING SUM(i.amount) > 0 ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Show the total number of active subscriptions for each customer, including their email and lifetime value, for customers who have at least one active subscription.
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, c.ltv, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS active_subscriptions FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id WHERE s.status = 'active' GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email, c.ltv HAVING COUNT(s.sub_id) > 0 ORDER BY active_subscriptions DESC

question: Show the total number of active subscriptions and total monthly revenue for each plan, along with the plan name.
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS total_subscriptions, SUM(p.price_monthly) AS total_revenue FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'trialing') GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Find the top 10 customers by lifetime value, along with their email and referral source, who have at least one active subscription.
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, c.referral_source, c.ltv FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'trialing') AND c.ltv IS NOT NULL GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email, c.referral_source, c.ltv ORDER BY c.ltv DESC LIMIT 10

question: For each customer, show their email, total number of shipments, and total amount paid, where the customer has at least one completed shipment.
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS total_shipments, SUM(i.amount) AS total_amount_paid FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN shipments sh ON s.sub_id = sh.sub_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE sh.delivered_date IS NOT NULL AND i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email ORDER BY total_amount_paid DESC

question: Find the total revenue generated by each plan in the last 30 days, along with the plan name and total number of new subscriptions.
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS total_new_subscriptions FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.issued_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' AND s.start_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Show the average lifetime value of customers who have at least one active subscription, grouped by referral source.
sql: SELECT c.referral_source, AVG(c.ltv) AS avg_ltv FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'trialing') AND c.ltv IS NOT NULL GROUP BY c.referral_source ORDER BY avg_ltv DESC

question: For each plan, find the total number of cancelled subscriptions and total amount refunded, where the subscription was cancelled in the last 60 days.
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS total_cancelled_subscriptions, SUM(i.amount) AS total_amount_refunded FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE s.end_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '60 days' AND s.status = 'cancelled' AND i.status IN ('refunded', 'partially_refunded') GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY total_amount_refunded DESC

question: Find the top 5 plans by total revenue generated in the last 90 days, along with the plan name and total number of new subscriptions.
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS total_new_subscriptions FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.issued_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days' AND s.start_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days' GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 5

question: For each plan, show the plan name, total number of subscriptions, and total monthly revenue generated by these subscriptions.
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS total_subscriptions, SUM(p.price_monthly) AS total_revenue FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'pending') GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Find the customers who have an average lifetime value above 1000 and show their email, referral source, and average lifetime value.
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, c.referral_source, AVG(c.ltv) AS avg_ltv FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email, c.referral_source HAVING AVG(c.ltv) > 1000 ORDER BY avg_ltv DESC

question: Which plans have a higher than average number of shipments per subscription and show the plan name, average shipments per subscription, and total shipments.
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, AVG(sh.shipment_id) AS avg_shipments, COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS total_shipments FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN shipments sh ON s.sub_id = sh.sub_id GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name HAVING AVG(sh.shipment_id) > (SELECT AVG(sh.shipment_id) FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN shipments sh ON s.sub_id = sh.sub_id) ORDER BY avg_shipments DESC

question: Show the total revenue generated by each customer through their subscriptions and the number of active subscriptions they have.
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS active_subscriptions FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'pending') AND i.status IN ('paid', 'pending') GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Find the plans with a higher than average customer lifetime value and show the plan name, average customer lifetime value, and total customer lifetime value.
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, AVG(c.ltv) AS avg_ltv, SUM(c.ltv) AS total_ltv FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name HAVING AVG(c.ltv) > (SELECT AVG(c.ltv) FROM customers c) ORDER BY avg_ltv DESC

question: For each customer, show their email, referral source, total number of subscriptions, and total revenue generated by these subscriptions in the last 30 days.
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, c.referral_source, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS total_subscriptions, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.issued_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' AND i.status IN ('paid', 'pending') GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email, c.referral_source ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Which customers have more than one active subscription and show their email, referral source, and number of active subscriptions.
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, c.referral_source, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS active_subscriptions FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'pending') GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email, c.referral_source HAVING COUNT(s.sub_id) > 1 ORDER BY active_subscriptions DESC

question: Find the total revenue generated by each plan through their subscriptions in the last 60 days and show the plan name, total revenue, and number of subscriptions.
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS total_subscriptions FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.issued_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '60 days' AND i.status IN ('paid', 'pending') GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Find the total revenue and number of paid invoices for each customer, considering only active subscriptions with a status of 'active' or 'pending' and invoices with a status of 'paid' or 'settled'
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, COUNT(i.invoice_id) AS paid_invoices, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'pending') AND i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Show the number of shipments and total revenue for each plan, including only subscriptions with a status of 'active' or 'pending' and shipments with a delivered date within the last 30 days
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS shipments, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN shipments sh ON s.sub_id = sh.sub_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'pending') AND sh.delivered_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Which customers have an average subscription price above 50, considering only active subscriptions with a status of 'active' or 'pending' and plans with a price monthly above 20
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, AVG(p.price_monthly) AS avg_subscription_price FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN plans p ON s.plan_id = p.plan_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'pending') AND p.price_monthly > 20 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email HAVING AVG(p.price_monthly) > 50 ORDER BY avg_subscription_price DESC

question: Find the total number of subscriptions and revenue for each billing cycle, including only subscriptions with a status of 'active' or 'pending' and invoices with a status of 'paid' or 'settled'
sql: SELECT p.billing_cycle, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS subscriptions, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'pending') AND i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY p.billing_cycle ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Show the number of customers and total revenue for each referral source, considering only customers with a signup date within the last 60 days and invoices with a status of 'paid' or 'settled'
sql: SELECT c.referral_source, COUNT(c.customer_id) AS customers, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE c.signup_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '60 days' AND i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY c.referral_source ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Find the average subscription price and number of subscriptions for each plan, including only subscriptions with a status of 'active' or 'pending' and plans with a price monthly above 10
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, AVG(p.price_monthly) AS avg_subscription_price, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS subscriptions FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'pending') AND p.price_monthly > 10 GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY avg_subscription_price DESC

question: Which plans have a total revenue above 1000, considering only subscriptions with a status of 'active' or 'pending' and invoices with a status of 'paid' or 'settled'
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'pending') AND i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name HAVING SUM(i.amount) > 1000 ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Show the total number of shipments and revenue for each customer, including only subscriptions with a status of 'active' or 'pending', shipments with a delivered date within the last 30 days, and invoices with a status of 'paid' or 'settled'
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS shipments, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN shipments sh ON s.sub_id = sh.sub_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'pending') AND sh.delivered_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' AND i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Which plans have the highest average lifetime value of customers, and what is the total number of customers and total revenue generated from each plan?
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, AVG(c.ltv) AS avg_ltv, COUNT(c.customer_id) AS total_customers, SUM(p.price_monthly) AS total_revenue FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE s.status IN ('active', 'completed') GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY avg_ltv DESC

question: Find the total number of shipments and total revenue generated by each customer in the last 30 days, along with their email and referral source.
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, c.referral_source, COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS total_shipments, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN shipments sh ON s.sub_id = sh.sub_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE sh.shipped_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' AND i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email, c.referral_source ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: Show the top 5 plans with the highest total revenue generated in the last quarter, along with the total number of subscriptions and average price.
sql: SELECT p.plan_id, p.name, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS total_subscriptions, AVG(p.price_monthly) AS avg_price FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.plan_id = s.plan_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id WHERE i.issued_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days' AND i.status IN ('paid', 'settled') GROUP BY p.plan_id, p.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 5

question: Which customers have the highest total lifetime value and have been subscribed for more than 6 months, along with their referral source and email?
sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, c.referral_source, c.ltv AS total_ltv FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id WHERE s.start_date <= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '180 days' AND s.status IN ('active', 'completed') ORDER BY c.ltv DESC
